original_hypothesis.title: Owner-Configurable taxCut Without Bounds Can Brick Core Flows via Division-by-Zero or Extreme Fee Skews
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Multiple contracts use taxCut as a divisor (NukeFund.receive, EntityForging.forgeWithListed devFee calculation, EntityTrading.buyNFT contribution calculation) but setters do not enforce taxCut > 0. Setting taxCut to 0 causes immediate division-by-zero reverts, bricking mint distribution, forging fee splits, and marketplace buys. Even non-zero extreme values can materially alter economics (e.g., taxCut=1 makes 100% go to devShare/contribution path).
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is an operationally common failure mode: a single mis-set parameter halts production. Even though it requires owner action (low realism under threat model), it is a realistic ops risk worth validating with runbooks/guards.
deployment_assumptions: Owner can accidentally or maliciously call setTaxCut(0) or extreme values
No off-chain governance controls prevent unsafe parameter changes
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: NukeFund.sol:setTaxCut(uint256), EntityForging.sol:setTaxCut(uint256), EntityTrading.sol:setTaxCut(uint256) have no require(_taxCut>0).

proof_of_signal.pattern: devShare = msg.value / taxCut; no guard that taxCut>0; setter lacks require

preconditions: Owner submits a transaction setting taxCut to 0 (or unsafe extreme)
Core flows continue to route ETH to NukeFund / calculate fee splits

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Critical divisor parameter lacks validation across multiple contracts

operational_questions: Are there operational controls (multisig policy, CI checks, change management) preventing unsafe taxCut updates?
Is taxCut expected to be fixed at 10 permanently (design intent)?
How would the team recover given pause/unpause is missing?
validation_requirements: Parameter-bound tests: taxCut=0 and taxCut=1 effects across mint/forge/trade
Operational review: owner key management and change approval process
Add monitoring for setTaxCut events and immediate revert-rate spikes

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: All mints/forges/trades that send ETH to NukeFund begin reverting
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Protocol effectively halts without an emergency pause
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Owner must restore taxCut to non-zero and communicate incident

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Full protocol DoS for revenue flows and marketplace/forging
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium; simple parameter fix but no pause and user trust impact
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Minting fund distribution, forging fee split, trading fee split

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: setTaxCut events; spike in reverted receive() calls at NukeFund

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
